statements.sql: SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)) AS ProductAlternateKey, ProductName AS EnglishProductName INTO silver.SelectIntoTarget FROM bronze.Product

refactor.extracted_sql: SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)) AS ProductAlternateKey, ProductName AS EnglishProductName FROM [bronze].[Product]
refactor.refactored_sql: WITH source_product AS ( SELECT * FROM [bronze].[Product] ), final AS ( SELECT CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25)) AS ProductAlternateKey, ProductName AS EnglishProductName FROM source_product ) SELECT * FROM final

source_tables.summary: Both queries read from [bronze].[Product]. The refactored version uses a CTE but sources the same table.

output_columns.summary: Both produce identical output columns: ProductAlternateKey (CAST(ProductID AS NVARCHAR(25))) and EnglishProductName (ProductName). Column order and types match.

joins.summary: Neither query contains joins. Both are single-table selects.

filters.summary: Neither query applies WHERE conditions. Both select all rows from the source.

aggregation_grain.summary: Neither query performs aggregation. Both operate at row level without GROUP BY.
